# FrigateRelay secret-scan fixture
#
# PURPOSE: Tripwire self-test. The 'tripwire-self-test' CI job greps ONLY this
# file to confirm every secret-scan regex still matches at least one line.
# If a pattern stops matching (e.g., regex was over-tightened), that job fails.
#
# IMPORTANT: These are FAKE credential-shaped strings. None are real credentials.
# DO NOT replace these values with "more realistic" examples — the synthetic
# nature (sequential chars, EXAMPLE suffixes, alg=none JWTs) is deliberate.
# The 'scan' CI job EXCLUDES this file; it is the TARGET in 'selftest' mode only.
#
# Each line is tagged   # secret-scan:fixture   so it is trivially greppable
# and obviously intentional in git log forever.

# Pattern 1 — Pushover AppToken (AppToken\s*=\s*[A-Za-z0-9]{20,})
AppToken=abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz012345 # secret-scan:fixture

# Pattern 2 — Pushover UserKey (UserKey\s*=\s*[A-Za-z0-9]{20,})
UserKey=ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ012345 # secret-scan:fixture

# Pattern 3 — RFC-1918 hard-coded IP (192\.168\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3})
server_url=http://192.168.99.99:8080/trigger # secret-scan:fixture

# Pattern 4 — Generic API key (api[Kk]ey\s*[=:]\s*["']?[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{20,})
# Two fixtures cover the optional-quote branch in both directions (double + single quotes)
# so the self-test exercises the full character class, not just one side.
apiKey = "xK9mR2nP4qT7vL1wY5sA8bD3cF6hJ0eG" # secret-scan:fixture
apiKey = 'zY8nX5mQ3pS6uH2jK9bF1wT4rL7vC0dE' # secret-scan:fixture

# Pattern 5 — Bearer token (Bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,})
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJub25lIiwidHlwIjoiSldUIn0.fake.signature # secret-scan:fixture

# Pattern 6 — GitHub PAT (ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36})
token=ghp_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ1234567890 # secret-scan:fixture

# Pattern 7 — AWS Access Key ID (AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16})
aws_access_key_id=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE00 # secret-scan:fixture
